# Manufacturing Operations Guide

## Supply Chain Resilience
Supply chain resilience is the capacity of a production network to absorb disruptions and continue operating. Companies build resilience by diversifying suppliers, holding buffer inventory, and improving visibility across transportation routes. Resilient planning supports continuity during shortages, shipping delays, and demand spikes.

## Quality Assurance
Quality assurance focuses on preventing defects and ensuring that products meet required standards. It includes incoming inspection, process controls, product testing, and continuous improvement. A reliable quality program improves trust, lowers waste, and protects brand reputation.

## Inventory Planning
Inventory planning balances service levels against capital costs. Good planning uses demand forecasts, reorder rules, and safety stock policies. A strong process reduces stockouts while avoiding excessive working capital tied up in inventory.
